SECURITY.md_missing_DOC_004.note: No SECURITY.md found in repo root. No security policy, vulnerability reporting process, or disclosure timeline documented.

issues: Title says 'pi-loop' but remote is ningtoba/infinite-loop, local dir is hermes-loop
References 480+ tests / 25+ test files — actual count is 838 tests across more files
Coverage recommendation uses pi_loop instead of hermes_loop
Mentions .env indirectly but project uses JSON config now
Section says 'run.sh' in project-structure tree but run.sh was removed
Unreleased section contains v14.33.0-era items (not truly unreleased — already shipped)
Lines like 'feat: implement SEC-001' and 'feat: auto-approve pi commands' mixed with old v14.x entries in [Unreleased]
SEC-001 in CHANGELOG refers to API-key auth middleware, but ENGINEERING_BACKLOG.md's SEC-001 refers to URL scheme validation — ID collision
No version bump section for latest changes (HEAD is beyond v14.39.0 mentions)

pi_version_requirement.note: README says 'pi coding agent' and shows 'pi --version' but states no minimum version. Users have no way to know if their pi is compatible.

other_issues: README title 'pi-loop' doesn't match repo name 'hermes-loop' or remote 'ningtoba/infinite-loop'
Install steps use 'pip install -e .' but pyproject.toml uses 'hermes-loop' package name

stale_hermes_branches_CLEAN_001.action_needed: git branch -d hermes/hermes-* (safe to delete, no remote tracking)
stale_hermes_branches_CLEAN_001.note: These were created via git worktree during hermes-era iterations. No remote hermes branches exist. Local branches merge with remote main — incorrect config.

stale_worktree_dirs_CLEAN_001.action_needed: git worktree remove .worktrees/hermes-*/ (after confirming no active use)
stale_worktree_dirs_CLEAN_001.note: These are git worktrees containing old hermes-era files (CHANGELOG.md 63KB, CONTRIBUTING.md 22KB, SKILL.md 88KB, etc.). All locked.

branch_structure.note: Remote is ningtoba/infinite-loop not nekophobia/hermes-loop. Local hermes branches track remote main instead of non-existent remote hermes branches.

remote_name_mismatch.issue: Local directory name doesn't match GitHub repo name

pre_commit_duality_TOOL_002.pre_commit_config_content: ruff (fix+format), trailing-whitespace, eof-fixer, check-json/yaml/toml, merge-conflict, detect-private-key, large-files
pre_commit_duality_TOOL_002.githooks_pre_commit_content: ruff check + ruff format --check on staged Python files only
pre_commit_duality_TOOL_002.duality_issue: Both co-exist with overlapping ruff checks but different scopes: .pre-commit-config.yaml runs on all files via pre-commit framework, .githooks/pre-commit runs only on staged Python files via bash.

githooks_README_mismatch.what_README_says: pre-commit hook regenerates shell completion scripts (scripts/completion/{bash,zsh})
githooks_README_mismatch.what_actual_hook_does: ruff check + ruff format --check on staged Python files

pre_commit_duality_TOOL_002.recommendation: Either update .githooks/README.md to match actual hook behavior, or update the hook to match the documented behavior.

py_typed_missing_TOOL_003.note: No pi_loop/py.typed marker file. PEP 561 packages need this for mypy to type-check users of the package. Without it, mypy assumes the package is untyped.

editorconfig_missing_TOOL_004.note: No .editorconfig. Different editors may use inconsistent indentation (ruff formatter catches this, but .editorconfig provides universal editor settings for all file types including non-Python).

env_in_gitignore_SEC_002.detail: .env, .env.local, .env.* are all in .gitignore. ENGINEERING_BACKLOG lists SEC-002 as 'Pending' but the issue is already fixed.
env_in_gitignore_SEC_002.note: Backlog SEC-002 status is stale — should be marked Done.

MEDIUM: blacklist:720 — urllib.request.urlopen(http_callback) with no scheme validation

SEC_001_http_callback_url_validation.issue: urllib.request.urlopen(http_callback) called without validating URL scheme. A file:// URL would read local files; data:// could trigger unexpected behavior (Bandit B310).
SEC_001_http_callback_url_validation.fix_needed: Add urlparse-based validation restricting schemes to http and https only. Reject file://, data://, ftp://, etc.

SEC_001_changelog_id_collision.issue: CHANGELOG uses SEC-001 for 'API-key authentication middleware' (implemented), ENGINEERING_BACKLOG uses SEC-001 for 'http_callback URL scheme validation' (pending). These are different fixes sharing the same ID.

SEC_005_shell_true_guarding.issue: subprocess.run(on_error_cmd, shell=True, timeout=30) where on_error_cmd is user-configurable via config.json. No command logging, no length/character restrictions, no documented risk.
SEC_005_shell_true_guarding.fix_needed: At minimum: (1) log command before execution at INFO level, (2) add length/character restrictions, (3) document risk in README, (4) warn on startup when configured.
